description:
You are a technical writer who maintains release-notes and versioning information for an internal documentation project that lives in /home/user/project. The project already contains the following two files: 1. /home/user/project/VERSION • Contains a single line with the current semantic version:
1.2.3 2. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md • Starts with a short header and one previous release entry. Your assignment: A. Perform a patch version bump from 1.2.3 to 1.2.4. • After you finish, the file /home/user/project/VERSION must contain exactly the single line (including a trailing newline character): 1.2.4 B. Prepend a new release entry at the top of /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md that follows exactly this template (automated tests are case- and spacing-sensitive): 
## [1.2.4] - YYYY-MM-DD
### Changed
- Updated documentation typos.
• Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s date in ISO-8601 format. • The existing content of CHANGELOG.md must remain unchanged below the new section. • Ensure there is a single blank line between the new section and the old content. C. Create an audit log file at /home/user/project/update.log. The file must contain exactly two lines, in this order: 
UPDATED VERSION to 1.2.4
PREPENDED CHANGELOG for 1.2.4
Constraints & Notes: • Do not change anything outside /home/user/project. • Keep line endings in Unix style (LF). • The entire task can be completed with just 3–4 simple shell commands; feel free to use any workflow you like. • No root privileges are available. • Once finished, ensure the three files reflect the required final state so that an automated test script can verify them verbatim.
truth:
Initial system state (before the agent starts): 1. Directory: /home/user/project 2. File /home/user/project/VERSION Contents (with trailing newline): 1.2.3\n 3. File /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md Contents: 
# Changelog

## [1.2.3] - 2023-06-15
### Added
- Initial release of the documentation set.
Expected final state (after correct completion): 1. /home/user/project/VERSION Contents: 1.2.4\n 2. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md Contents must start with: 
## [1.2.4] - <today's ISO date>
### Changed
- Updated documentation typos.
followed by a single blank line and then the original contents beginning with # Changelog exactly as they were. 3. /home/user/project/update.log Contents (each line ends with LF): UPDATED VERSION to 1.2.4\n PREPENDED CHANGELOG for 1.2.4\n
